title: Pilocereus chrysostele
taxonomy: Pilocereus chrysostele is classified within the family Cactaceae and the subfamily Cactoideae. It belongs to the tribe Pachycereeae, which is a group of epiphytic and tree-like cacti. This species is closely related to Pilocereus pseudopachycladus and Pilocereus glaucochrous. The genus Pilocereus is a part of the broader group of cacti that are epiphytic in nature, meaning they grow on other plants rather than in the soil. Pilocereus chrysostele is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
